rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 22, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT CLICKSYNDICATE.COM, LLC, FAIRLIGHT SCIENTIFIC, LLC, ALTARE PUBLISHING, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. Defendants' motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted. For efficiency's sake, the motion will be treated under CCP §426.30. The text of the proposed cross-complaint makes clear its bases. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: COAST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY VS. CHRISTIAN ARTURO RUIZ ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 22, 2019, Line 5, PLAINTIFF COAST NATIONAL INSURANCE COMPANY's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. GRANTED IN PART. Debtor Ruiz to remit $60 per pay period to service the judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. If the tentative is not contested, the court will execute a judicial council order confirming the tentative. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MOTIVA ENTERPRISES LLC A DELAWRE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. AXIS SURPLUS INSURANCE COMPANY AN ILLINOIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Motiva Enterprises Llc'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 22, 2019, Line 8, Plaintiff Motiva Enterprises Llc'S Motion For Leave To File An Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Motiva Enterprises LLC's motion for leave to file an amended complaint is GRANTED. California has "a policy of great liberality in permitting amendments to the complaint at any stage of the proceedings." (Atkinson v. Elk Corp. (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 739, 761.) The supposed delay in bringing this motion and the shortened time to conduct discovery are not unduly prejudicial to defendants National Fire & Marine Insurance Company and Maxum Indemnity Company. This department is not authorized to grant trial continuances, which must be sought from department 206. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER SARKANY ET AL VS. CHRISTIE WEST ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendant Christie West
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 22, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER SARKANY, RAMSEY ABOUREMELEH, SANDRA FIERRO, NINA ROBINS' Motion For Leave To Conduct Financial Discovery On Defendant Christie West. Pro Tem Judge Maxwell Pritt, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Please come prepared to address the following issues: the weight of the evidence submitted in support of and opposition to the motion; whether the evidence supports a finding that the plaintiffs are "very likely" to prevail on their punitive damages claim, Jabro v. Superior Court, 95 Cal. App. 4th 754, 758 (2002); whether the court must also find that there is a substantial probability that the plaintiffs will prevail on their underlying claims; whether the Court can grant leave to conduct financial discovery without a protective order governing the disclosure of such financial information; whether defendant's admission that she has "made derogatory comments to plaintiffs" on occasion is sufficient to constitute a violation of SFRO 37.10(B) or to constitute constructive conviction; whether punitive damages have been imposed in other cases based on a landlord's derogatory comments to a tenant; whether the Court can consider judicially noticed facts or any facts set forth in the declaration submitted with Plaintiffs' reply brief in weighing the evidence submitted in support of and opposition to the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mpritt@bsfllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
